results.question: Do genetically identical cells behave differently?

min_criteria.criterion: M1: names a specific per-cell quantity (a low-copy protein count or growth rate), not 'behavior'
min_criteria.rationale: Names per-seed growth_rate (1/h) as the primary per-cell quantity, an in-model observable.

min_criteria.criterion: M2: predicts persistent between-cell variance > 0 vs an isogenic baseline
min_criteria.rationale: H1 predicts across-seed CV of growth_rate for identical wildtype/basal exceeds noise floor by >=3x — a between-seed variance > 0 vs isogenic ensemble.

min_criteria.criterion: M3: states a falsifier (uniform behavior, or between-cell variance == within-cell noise)
min_criteria.rationale: Explicit falsifier: CV within its own bootstrap CI (spread == numerical noise floor) refutes; concrete refuting numbers given.

min_criteria.criterion: M4: design is an isogenic replicate ensemble, in-envelope
min_criteria.rationale: seeds=50 replicate runs of identical wildtype/basal design — an isogenic ensemble, in-model.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S1: gives a between-cell CV and/or predicts CV proportional to 1/sqrt(copy number)
stringent_criteria.rationale: Provides a between-cell CV metric (with illustrative ~4% refuting value) and predicts CV increases when ribosome/rRNA pool redundancy is reduced — a copy-number/1-over-sqrt-N-style dependence, though not stated as an exact 1/sqrt scaling.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S2: names a variance-partition / autocorrelation test separating persistent individuality from fast noise
stringent_criteria.rationale: Uses a numerical-noise-floor control and bootstrap CI, but no true variance-partition or autocorrelation test separating persistent between-cell individuality from fast within-run noise; matched-generation CV is not a persistence/autocorrelation analysis. The residual ambiguities concede this.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S3: the predicted result discriminates non-genetic individuality from the genetic and measurement-noise rivals
stringent_criteria.rationale: Discriminates against solver/measurement-noise artifact via the dial, but does not exclude genetic differences (design is isogenic so moot) nor the deterministic-chaos rival, which the hypothesis itself admits is unaddressed. Discrimination is incomplete.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S4: ties the variance to a specific low-copy protein pool (the isolating cause)
stringent_criteria.rationale: Ties variance to ribosome/rRNA operon dosage (an abundant, high-copy translation resource), not a specific low-copy signalling protein pool as the isolating cause; the mechanism invoked is opposite to the canonical low-copy-number Poisson origin.

results.comment: Solid on minimum criteria: names growth_rate as per-cell observable, isogenic seed ensemble, predicts excess between-seed variance, and gives a crisp falsifier with a manipulable dial. Weaker on stringency: it relies on a numerical-noise-floor control rather than a variance-partition/autocorrelation persistence test, does not exclude the deterministic-chaos rival (self-acknowledged), and anchors the causal mechanism to the high-copy ribosome/rRNA pool rather than the canonical low-copy signalling protein pool. Reasonable CV-based operationalization but misses the low-copy-number isolating cause central to Spudich & Koshland.

hypothesis.question: Do genetically identical cells behave differently?
hypothesis.claim: Within the whole-cell simulator, independent stochastic seeds of the identical wildtype/basal design produce reproducible, above-noise-floor divergence in single-cell growth_rate trajectories — a model-level phenomenon of emergent seed-to-seed variability in this in-silico system (not a claim about biological single-molecule expression noise, which the instrument cannot directly observe). This divergence should be large relative to a calibrated numerical-noise control, and should scale characteristically with a manipulable stochastic-capacity dial (ribosome/rRNA operon dosage), giving a falsifiable signature that distinguishes genuine seed-driven kinetic divergence from mere solver/discretization artifact.
hypothesis.h1: Across seeds=50 replicate runs of wildtype/basal, (a) across-seed CV of growth_rate at matched generation index exceeds a pre-registered numerical-noise-floor estimate by >=3x, AND (b) this excess variance is sensitive to a genetic dial known to modulate expression/translation noise capacity: rrna_operon_knockout (partial rRNA operon deletion, reducing ribosome pool redundancy) increases across-seed growth_rate CV further by >=50% relative to wildtype/basal, consistent with a stochastic-kinetic origin rather than a fixed solver artifact (a pure artifact should NOT respond systematically to a biological dial).
hypothesis.h0: Across-seed growth_rate CV in wildtype/basal is statistically indistinguishable from the pre-registered numerical noise-floor estimate (obtained from a seeds-only re-run of a deterministic-equivalent control, i.e. minimal-noise integration setting if available, else repeated identical-seed reruns), AND rrna_operon_knockout produces no systematic change in across-seed CV (welch_t < 2, effect_pct within +/-10% of wildtype/basal CV) — i.e. seed-to-seed spread is a fixed artifact of the numerics, not a phenomenon that tracks a biological noise-generating dial.

operational_defs.construct: seed-to-seed phenotypic divergence (model-level, not biological single-cell noise)
operational_defs.measure: per-seed instantaneous growth_rate (1/h) time series across seeds=50 of wildtype/basal; compute across-seed CV at matched generation index; report per-seed spread

across-seed CV of growth_rate under a control design intended to minimize biological stochastic sources while preserving numerical solver behavior (same wildtype/basal design, seeds=50) used as the reference distribution against which excess CV is judged relative to its own bootstrap confidence interval, rather than an arbitrary fixed threshold

across-seed CV of growth_rate under rrna_operon_knockout (partial rRNA operon deletion, seeds=50) compared to wildtype/basal CV; ratio and welch_t of the two CV distributions (bootstrapped per-seed resampling)

per-seed ppgpp_conc trace; cross-seed spike-timing correlation and amplitude relative to rolling pre-spike local baseline (window = preceding 5 timepoints per seed), reported as secondary/corroborating evidence, not primary falsifier

hypothesis.predicted_effect: Direction: across-seed growth_rate CV in wildtype/basal exceeds the bootstrapped noise-floor CI substantially (effect_z_vs_corpus >= 3), and rrna_operon_knockout further increases CV by >=50% relative to wildtype/basal (welch_t >= 2). Magnitude anchored to bootstrap CI of the null's own resampled distribution, not an arbitrary fixed percentage, closing the prior no-man's-land gap.

falsifier.decision_rule: Compute across-seed CV of growth_rate (matched generation index) for both wildtype/basal and rrna_operon_knockout. Bootstrap 1000 resamples of wildtype/basal's own 50 seeds to build a null CI for CV. If wildtype/basal CV falls within its own bootstrap CI (i.e., not distinguishable from a stable estimate, meaning no excess beyond sampling variability) OR if rrna_operon_knockout CV is not significantly greater than wildtype/basal CV (welch_t < 2, effect_pct < 10%), the hypothesis is refuted -- the observed spread would then be a fixed property of the numerics/sampling, insensitive to a biological noise-modulating dial.
falsifier.refuting_result: wildtype/basal growth_rate CV = 4% (within bootstrap CI of itself, effect_z_vs_corpus = 0.4), and rrna_operon_knockout CV = 4.3% (welch_t = 0.6, effect_pct = 7% relative to wildtype/basal) -- i.e., reducing ribosome pool redundancy does not amplify seed-to-seed divergence, indicating the observed variability is a fixed numerical/sampling artifact, not a phenomenon tracking a biological stochasticity-generating dial.

residual_ambiguities: [unfalsifiable] The candidate object for this round is empty ({}); there is no claim, h1, h0, operational_defs, predicted_effect, or falsifier to assess. An empty candidate cannot be falsified, confirmed, or even parsed as a hypothesis — there is nothing to raise or lower the risk of failing.

[hidden_auxiliary] The moderator's resolution to both prior irreducible ambiguities ('operationalize onto the single most directly measurable in-model observable; prefer the named molecular quantity, else growth_rate') is a generic meta-rule, not an actual answer to either specific question asked (full-trajectory vs rolling ppGpp baseline; raw vs detrended growth_rate variance for the diffusive test). Without the candidate re-instantiating operational_defs that apply this rule concretely, it remains unclear how baseline computation and detrending are actually implemented, leaving both original measurement-construction ambiguities unresolved in practice.

[rival_not_excluded] Carried over unresolved from round 2: the central rival explanation — that observed seed-to-seed divergence (whether flat, growing, or dosage-sensitive) could reflect deterministic chaotic/Lyapunov sensitivity-amplification rather than genuine stochastic-kinetic noise — was never excluded, and the discriminating test's own logic conceded that chaos with a long transient could reproduce the 'growing variance' signature. Since no new candidate was given this round, this rival remains fully live and unaddressed.

[hidden_auxiliary] Carried over unresolved from rounds 1-2: none of the magnitude thresholds proposed in prior rounds (e.g., CV>=15%, 50% CV increase, 30% CV increase, 2x SD growth, welch_t>=2) have ever been derived from an independent calibration, analytic argument, or prior simulation baseline. Any future re-instantiation of these thresholds without justification reintroduces the same arbitrary-cutoff problem.

results.question: What does a cell do when it suddenly runs out of amino acids?

min_criteria.criterion: M1: names ppGpp concentration and rRNA/ribosome or growth rate as observables
min_criteria.rationale: The structured fields (claim, operational_defs, predicted_effect) are all empty; only residual-ambiguity text mentions ppgpp_conc, and there is no coherent stated hypothesis naming ribosome/growth as the response observable.

min_criteria.criterion: M2: predicts ppGpp up and ribosome/growth down on downshift vs a steady-state baseline
min_criteria.rationale: No predicted_effect is stated anywhere; the direction of ppGpp and ribosome/growth change is never asserted.

min_criteria.criterion: M3: states a falsifier (no ppGpp rise, or ribosome synthesis unchanged despite a spike)
min_criteria.rationale: The falsifier field is null and no falsification condition is articulated.

min_criteria.criterion: M4: design is an amino-acid downshift, in-envelope
min_criteria.rationale: candidate_designs is empty; a withdrawal run is alluded to only obliquely in ambiguity notes, but no actual design specifying an amino-acid downshift is provided.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S1: gives the sign and rough magnitude/timescale of the ppGpp spike and ribosome drop
stringent_criteria.rationale: No sign, magnitude, or timescale is provided; predicted_effect is empty.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S2: specifies a pre/post (time-resolved) comparison statistic
stringent_criteria.rationale: No comparison statistic is defined; only vague references to a 'null model' appear in ambiguity text without specification.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S3: the prediction discriminates the active-ppGpp-signal from the passive-depletion rival
stringent_criteria.rationale: The rivals field is empty and no prediction is stated, so no discrimination against passive depletion is operationalized despite passing mention.

stringent_criteria.criterion: S4: includes the ppGpp-null (relA/spoT knockout) relaxed-phenotype control to prove causality
stringent_criteria.rationale: The relA/spoT double-knockout is mentioned only in an ambiguity note critiquing it, not as an actual specified control arm in a design; no relaxed-phenotype prediction is made.

results.comment: The hypothesis is essentially empty: all core structured fields (claim, H1, H0, operational_defs, predicted_effect, falsifier, rivals, candidate_designs) are blank. Only the residual_ambiguities section contains substantive content, and it discusses design elements (relA/spoT knockout, glucose ramp control, ppGpp demotion) meta-critically rather than actually asserting them. The generator failed to converge and produced no gradable scientific claim. It touches the right biology (ppGpp, RelA/SpoT, tRNA charging) in passing but never commits to any observable, direction, falsifier, or design.

hypothesis.question: What does a cell do when it suddenly runs out of amino acids?
residual_ambiguities: [hidden_auxiliary] The pre-registration rule for n ('median n across 3 basal-condition replicate seeds, logged before the withdrawal run executes') still does not specify what data within those seeds is fit to obtain n (which timepoints, which loss function, whether fraction_trna_charged and growth_rate are both drawn from true basal steady-state or from some transient), nor how sensitive the H1/H0 verdict is to reasonable variation in this fitting procedure. Three seeds is also a very small calibration sample; the median could be unstable, and no tolerance/robustness check is specified.

[hidden_auxiliary] The relA/spoT double-knockout arm is proposed to rule out SpoT compensation, but the design does not include a pre-shift baseline-fitness check (growth_rate, ribosome_conc, protein_mass, etc.) for THIS double mutant analogous to the one implicitly needed for relA_knockout alone. A double knockout of relA and spoT is likely to have substantially different basal physiology (spoT null strains are often growth-impaired or require suppressor mutations in real biology), so any 'flattening' of ppgpp_conc could reflect a sick, generally perturbed cell rather than clean removal of the RelA/SpoT synthesis axis.

[rival_not_excluded] The orthogonal glucose-ramp-down control tests whether relA_knockout is generically less responsive, but glucose downshift in real physiology also engages ppGpp (via SpoT) and stringent-like responses, so a specific-vs-generic dissociation may not be cleanly available: if relA_knockout DOES show a blunted response to glucose ramp-down too, the candidate has not specified whether that result would be interpreted as 'generic dampening' or 'expected because SpoT/ppGpp is also involved in glucose downshift' -- i.e., the control's own decisive-test logic has a rival explanation not addressed.

[construct_ambiguity] The claim states it 'resolves the tie by DEMOTING ppgpp_conc to the primary OUTCOME... using fraction_trna_charged purely as the INPUT to a pre-registered passive-depletion null model.' But the user's actual resolution text, repeated verbatim across all three rounds, still says only 'prefer the named molecular quantity, else growth_rate' without disambiguating which named molecular quantity. The candidate is treating an unresolved tie as if it had been decisively broken in ppGpp's favor; this remains a genuine open choice the user has not actually made.
